results.title: URSA Cine 17K
results.credit_line: Courtesy Blackmagic Design, Major Partner of ACMI
results.brief_description:
The Blackmagic URSA Cine 17K 65 brings large-format 65mm filmmaking within reach of more creators. Traditionally associated with epic cinema, 65mm refers to a larger image area than standard 35mm film, allowing for greater detail, depth and scale. Here, that cinematic format is reimagined as a digital camera system.

The 17K sensor captures images with extraordinary resolution, suitable for giant IMAX-style screens or cropped for cinema and television formats. Just as significant is its integrated production workflow. With fast, high-capacity storage built into the camera and high-speed networking for on-set media sync, it can record small proxy files alongside original media and share footage with editors while shooting continues.

This camera was used to shoot HBO’s Rooster (2026), starring Steve Carell, and reflects Blackmagic Design’s ambition to make high-end production more seamless and accessible.

results.title: PYXIS 12K
results.credit_line: Courtesy Blackmagic Design, Major Partner of ACMI
results.brief_description:
The Blackmagic PYXIS 12K is a compact box camera designed for flexibility. Made from precision CNC-machined aerospace aluminium, it is lightweight, strong and highly riggable. It can be mounted to drones, built into larger camera rigs, or adapted for documentary, commercial, feature film and personal projects.

Released in 2025, the PYXIS 12K brings high-resolution digital cinematography into a smaller, more customisable form. Its 12K sensor captures extremely detailed images, giving filmmakers more freedom to crop, stabilise or reframe footage. Its modular design allows filmmakers to shape the camera around each shoot, rather than working around a fixed camera body.

Its appeal lies in that combination: Hollywood-level resolution in a camera built for independent, professional and experimental workflows.
